sections.label: Lyrics
lines: Hand I Can’t See
I drew a roadmap out of fear and habit,
Quiet pencil marks across the grain.
But every mile turned into static,
Every hope felt weathered by the rain.
The forest gates were cold and hollow,
Branches writing warnings in the air.
Then a still, small voice began to follow,
Like a lantern held by hands I couldn’t see were there.
And when the night began to press too close,
You spoke the kind of truth that darkness knows.
So I trust the Hand I cannot see,
Even when my chest feels tight.
You straighten rivers under me,
Clear a path beneath the night.
In all my steps, in all my grief,
Your whisper keeps my pulse aligned.
I trust the Hand I cannot see
In the winter of my mind.
I chased the warmth of old predictions,
thinking fate would meet me where I stood.
But the world rewrote its own inscriptions,
and broke me open for my good.
Some fears grew roots in broken places,
Some tears carved valleys through my skin.
Yet somehow You turned ruins into graces,
Proof that healing always starts within.
And when the wind began to blur the trail,
You stilled my breath and taught me not to fail.
So I trust the Hand I cannot see,
Even when my chest feels tight.
You straighten rivers under me,
Clear a path beneath the night.
In all my steps, in all my grief,
Your whisper keeps my pulse aligned.
I trust the Hand I cannot see
In the winter of my mind.
You take the echoes from the hollow places,
Shape them into songs I never wrote.
You lift the frost from all the barren spaces,
Tie hope like thread around my throat.
Every broken branch becomes confession,
Every shadow grows a sign
That You’re the quiet, unseen lesson
Turning darkness into spine.
So I trust the Hand I cannot see,
Even when the world feels cold.
When every road looks lost to me,
You draw a line through dust and soul.
In all my days, in all my aching,
Your guidance moves beneath the time.
I trust the Hand I cannot see
In the winter of my mind.
